steps.title: Find the key signal in the family structure
steps.content: The passage shows black swan families successfully rear cygnets in two different configurations: standard female-male pairs AND male-male pairs, with male-male pairs achieving even higher survival rates. This reveals a structure that adapts to different forms and succeeds in each.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the structural flexibility
steps.content: ”Elasticity” denotes the capacity to bend, stretch, and change form while maintaining resilience and function. The passage demonstrates this through successful female-male and male-male pair arrangements, with the alternative form achieving superior outcomes—showing the system adapts and thrives in multiple configurations. Cohesion, affection, and reciprocity describe unity/bonding, emotional connection, and mutual exchange respectively—none capture this adaptive flexibility. That matches choice B — B is correct.
